doc_title: A Neural Network Architecture Combining Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) and Support Vector Machine (SVM) for Intrusion Detection in Network Traffic Data

items.fact: Mukkamala, Janoski, & Sung (2002)[17] shows how support vector machine (SVM) and artificial neural network (ANN) can be used to accomplish the said task.
items.evidence_span: A study by Mukkamala, Janoski, \& Sung (2002)[17] shows how support vector machine (SVM) and artificial neural network (ANN) can be used to accomplish the said task.

items.fact: Alalshekmubarak & Smith[2] proposed an approach combining ANN and SVM for time-series classification.
items.evidence_span: An approach combining ANN and SVM was proposed by Alalshekmubarak \& Smith[2], for time-series classification.

the present paper proposes to use SVM as the classifier in a neural network architecture. Specifically, a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) RNN (see Figure 1). Similar to the work of Alalshekmubarak \& Smith (2013)[2]
Similar to the work of Alalshekmubarak \& Smith (2013)[2] and Tang (2013)[22], the present paper proposes to use SVM as the classifier in a neural network architecture.
This finding corroborates the claims by Alalshekmubarak \& Smith (2013)[2]
